developer.title: {{brand}} API: getting started
developer.subtitle: New to APIs? Here is how to connect your website, blog, or app to our shows database in minutes.
developer.sectionWhatIntro: An API is a bridge between our site and yours. Your tools can read our data in real time. Here are three examples:
developer.uc1Title: No more double entry (theatres and companies)
developer.uc1Body: Got a WordPress site? Instead of copying tour dates manually on your site and on {{brand}}, the API lets your site read our calendar. Change a date on {{brand}} and it updates on your site.
developer.uc2Before: Writing about a play? Use our API to show a small
developer.uc2After: widget so readers know which theatres are hosting it.
developer.uc3Body: Tired of Lorem Ipsum for assignments? Use our API for apps with real cultural data (schedules, venue maps…) and strengthen your portfolio.
developer.tutorialIntro: To query our server you cannot just open the URL in a browser: you must prove you are allowed. Follow these three steps:
developer.step1P1: This key is your passport. Never share it publicly (e.g. on GitHub).
developer.step1LoginAfter: to your account to generate a key.
developer.step1LoggedAfter: , you are signed in! Create your key now:
developer.step2P1a: To authenticate, add your key to the request header (
developer.step2P2: Pick the language that fits your project and copy the code:
developer.step3Quote: "What are the exact URLs? What data will I get?"
developer.step3P2b: is for: a visual UI that lists our endpoints and lets you try them live. Here is how:
developer.instr1: 1. Authorize: open Swagger from the link below. Top right, click the green "Authorize" button.
developer.instr2: 2. Your key: a dialog opens. Paste your key (sk_…) in the field, click "Authorize", then close ("Close").
developer.instr3: 3. Open an endpoint: click a row (e.g. GET /api/shows) to expand it.
developer.instr4: 4. Try it: click "Try it out", then "Execute". The server response appears below.

auth.passwordRules: 8 chars min, 1 uppercase, 1 lowercase, 1 digit, 1 special (@$!%*?&)

apiKeys.title: Manage my API keys
apiKeys.description: Use these keys to authenticate requests to our public API.
apiKeys.nameRequired: Please enter a name for the key.
apiKeys.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete this key? It will stop working immediately.
apiKeys.emptyList: You do not have any API keys yet.
apiKeys.docWhatBody: This key acts like a secret password. It lets other sites (your showcase site, a blog, or a partner app) connect securely to our platform to fetch show information automatically.
apiKeys.docHowRegularBody: Copy your key above and paste it into the “API key” field of the tool you want to connect.
apiKeys.docHowDevBody: Include this key in the HTTP header
apiKeys.docFooterBefore: 👉 Explore all available data in our
